'''Cuirilla''' was a celestial body<ref name="CRO">''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]''</ref> located in the [[Cuirilla system/Legends|Cuirilla system]], a part of the [[Mbandamonte sector/Legends|Mbandamonte sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[Trailing Sectors/Legends|Trailing Sectors]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It shared its name with the [[Spacetug/Legends|tug]] model known as the [[VR-10 Cuirilla-Rayl Xylines-class spacetug|VR-10 ''Cuirilla-Rayl Xylines''-class spacetug]].<ref name="Abduction">{{WEGCite|story=The Abduction|book=Classic Adventures|stext=The Abduction of Crying Dawn Singer}}</ref> During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], at some point by the [[Time/Legends|time]] shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], [[Lavek Talstin]], the [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Action VI bulk transport/Legends|Action VI transport]] [[Wanderer (Action IV transport)|''Wanderer'']], operated from Cuirilla—a fact that Alliance [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] eventually noted in an entry dedicated to Talstin that was part of a [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] detailing various Rebel operatives.<ref name="CRO" />

==Behind the scenes==
Cuirilla was created by [[Chuck Truett]] and was introduced in ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', a [[1994]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook published for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="CRO" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Cuirilla [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the celestial body, in grid square N-15.<ref name="TEA" />
